rules_evaluated.rule_lead: **No stale markers, no "fixed" retrospectives, no session biography.**

rules_evaluated.evidence: line 14: "Real code, real tests, real history" — Boris voice strong; line 11: conversational framing — register appropriate throughout

rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Not an article draft; student-facing prework

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: **Credit the original when paraphrasing a named technique.**
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No named techniques requiring credit in body region

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: **Always "you", never "the student"; teacher framing stays out of body.**
rules_evaluated.evidence: lines 7,11,17,25: second-person throughout; imperative directions stay student-focused; no teacher framing in body

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: **Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body.**
**Cap practitioner attribution at one student-side mention per module.**
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No practitioner attribution in body region

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: **Punchy framing keeps its punch; carve-out lives downstream.**
**Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + project-level strategy docs, NOT in student-facing curriculum body.**
rules_evaluated.evidence: lines 13-15: bullets are operational criteria for repo selection (real code, tests, history; size of work), not positioning/defensive language

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: **Watch out for planting jargon too early.**
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 54: 'Install these as personal Claude Code skills' earns procedurally in context before use; placeholder $CONTENT_URL is build-time carve-out per maintainer note line 86

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: **Audit the closer when trimming a body-prose forcing function.**
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No visible prose trims leaving dangling references

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: **No combative verbs about the agent in body prose.**
rules_evaluated.evidence: verbs: Fire up, Open, Hand, Ask, Confirm, Bring — all peer-flat or warm
line 36: <CONTENT_URL> is build-time substitution with explicit carve-out per maintainer block and check_prompts.md rule text

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: **Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.**
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 30: 'Ask Claude to fetch the content tarball to ~/Downloads.' provides semantic action summary; line 45: Step 4 prompt also has lead-in
